sample.title: Trump dismisses victories by Mamdani-backed New York Democrats
sample.description:
After New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani scored three major primary wins in his attempt to remake the Democratic Party into a democratic socialist force, President Trump dismissed the victories.

sample.title: Venezuela quake victim rescued from rubble in Caracas
sample.description:
Emergency workers rescued a victim of Venezuela's earthquake from the rubble of a collapsed building after strong earthquakes struck west of Venezuela's capital, toppling buildings and trapping people in the rubble.

sample.title: LIVE: Strong quakes topple buildings in Caracas
sample.description:
Strong earthquakes struck west of Venezuela's capital, toppling buildings in Caracas, trapping people in the rubble and prompting scientists to warn of potentially heavy casualties and widespread destruction across the South American country.

Follow our live coverage: https://reut.rs/3QzvYqj

#earthquake #Venezuela #Caracas #rescue #quake #live #Reuters #news

sample.title: Congress tells Trump to end Iran war, but will it matter? #BBCNews
First responders at collapsed building site after twin earthquakes hit Caracas, Venezuela. #BBCNews
Powerful back-to-back earthquakes strike Venezuela | BBC News
sample.description:
Two major earthquakes shook the Venezuelan capital of Caracas seconds apart on Wednesday evening.

The first 7.2-magnitude quake was centred in the state of Carabobo, some 20km from the capital, Caracas.

It was followed by a stronger earthquake just 39 seconds later, with that one registering a 7.5 magnitude, the US Geological Survey says.
 
Buildings have collapsed as residents poured out into the streets, and the interior minister has asked people to leave their homes.

It is still unclear how many casualties there have been and what the extent of the damage will be.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#Venezuela #BBCNews

sample.title: John Kerry on how to negotiate with Iran | The Security Brief
sample.description:
The former US Secretary of State John Kerry sits down with The Security Brief - to discuss his firsthand experience of negotiating with Iran, resulting in the 2015 Iran nuclear deal. 
 
Was the JCPOA as flawed as Donald Trump has claimed? And what can the Trump administration learn from past US experiences? 
 
Plus on NATO, Secretary Kerry also tells us what Russia's Vladimir Putin told him about a conversation on US missiles positioned in Europe. 
 
The Security Brief is presented by Christian Fraser and Mikey Kay. 

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#JohnKerry #US #Iran #BBCNews
